name: Grant Proposal - NIH R01 Alzheimer's Research

inputs.research_topic: Microglial Immunometabolism in Alzheimer's Disease Progression
inputs.keywords: microglia, immunometabolism, neuroinflammation, Alzheimer's disease, glycolysis, oxidative phosphorylation, metabolic reprogramming, TREM2, APOE4, neurodegeneration
inputs.additional_information: Funding target: NIH R01 (National Institute on Aging), $2.5M direct costs over 5 years, application deadline in 4 months. Preliminary data: We have RNA-seq data showing distinct metabolic profiles in TREM2-deficient vs wild-type microglia, preliminary evidence that metabolic reprogramming precedes neurodegeneration in mouse models, proof-of-concept showing metabolic modulators can shift microglial phenotype. Research aims: (1) Define metabolic signatures of disease-associated microglia in human AD brains using spatial metabolomics, (2) Determine causal role of microglial metabolic dysfunction in AD progression using conditional knockout mice, (3) Test therapeutic potential of metabolic interventions (metformin, 2-DG) in APP/PS1 AD mouse models. Innovation: First to link microglial metabolism directly to AD pathology, novel spatial metabolomics approach, potential new therapeutic avenue. Significance: Could explain why anti-inflammatory approaches fail in AD, provide druggable metabolic targets. Team: PI (immunology + neurodegeneration expertise), Co-I (metabolism specialist), collaborators at NIH for human tissue access. Need: Compelling proposal narrative, detailed methodology, realistic budget with justification, strong significance/innovation section, preliminary data presentation, critical review against NIH scoring criteria.
